# P4 Instrument Catalog — accuracy review

**Applied 2026-07-03** as migration `p4_instruments_catalog` (repo copy: `db/seed_p4_instruments.sql`, generated by `game/tools/gen_instruments.py`). `data_version` → 64. **61 new generic devices**, bringing the catalog to **72 devices across all 19 `device_class` values**. Idempotent (`on conflict (id) do update`).

## What these rows are — and are NOT

Per the accuracy contract (spec §1, §8), each device field carries a data class:

| Field(s) | Class | How it was filled here |
|---|---|---|
| `french_size`, `od_inches`, `id_inches`, `length_cm`, `tip_shape`, `coating`, `material` | **CITED** (IFU-sourced) | **NOT yet cited.** Class-typical *nominal* values only. `spec_source` on every row reads *"Class-typical nominal values (X-type); physical dimensions PENDING manufacturer IFU (Ryan to attach) — treat as MODELED until cited."* This mirrors the P2 device precedent. **Nothing here is presented in-game as a cited dimension.** |
| `stiffness`, `lubricity`, `trackability`, `support`, `visibility` (1–10) | **MODELED** | Authored by me with a required `modeled_basis` note on each row explaining the rating. Navigation scales are left `NULL` for devices where they don't apply (coils, plugs, particles, embolics, filters). |
| `tier` (1–3), `price_funds` | **DESIGN** | Game economy only. Tiers follow the existing zero-sum ladder; prices scaled to the current economy (tier 1 ~300–900, tier 2 ~1200–3800, tier 3 ~4200–6000). |

All devices are `manufacturer = 'generic'` and named "X-type" — no real brand is asserted as fact. The Spec Bench shows the PENDING label verbatim, so the game never displays these as CITED.

## Coverage (by class)

needle 7 · guidewire 6 · micro_guidewire 4 · catheter 8 · sheath 6 · microcatheter 4 · balloon 5 · stent 4 · stent_graft 3 · coil 3 · plug 2 · particle 5 · liquid_embolic 3 · closure 2 · drain 5 · filter 1 · snare 2 · port 1 · other 1.

New families include: biopsy/access needles (Chiba, core, FNA, coaxial), support wires (Amplatz/Rosen/Lunderquist), reverse-curve selectives (SOS, Simmons-2, RIM), flush catheters (pigtail, omni), sheaths (5–8F, radial, long braided guide), microcatheters (2.1–2.8F incl. DMSO-compatible), PTA/HP/DCB/occlusion balloons, self-/balloon-expanding + biliary + venous stents, covered stents incl. a TIPS endoprosthesis, pushable/detachable/packing coils, vascular plugs, PVA/calibrated-sphere/DEB/gelfoam particles, n-BCA/EVOH/STS liquid embolics, suture & collagen-plug closure, all-purpose/nephrostomy/biliary drains, retrievable IVC filter, and gooseneck/multi-loop snares.

## Verification done

- 72 devices, 19 classes, 65 PENDING-IFU; `v_game_ready` unchanged (1); chest-port 7-device kit intact → economy/required-kit untouched.
- Security advisors: only the 2 known pre-existing WARNs (`is_admin` SECURITY DEFINER — intentional; leaked-password toggle). No new findings from this additive change.
- Game test suite green (no code changed this phase; shop/sim-lab render published devices by query, so all 72 appear automatically).

## Your to-dos (Ryan)

1. **Attach IFUs** to replace `spec_source` PENDING notes with real dimensions + source URLs (flips each row's physical spec from MODELED-until-cited → CITED). The dashboard device editor supports this.
2. **Adjust any MODELED 1–10 scales** you disagree with (each has a `modeled_basis` note to argue against).
3. **Tune DESIGN prices/tiers** for balance if desired.
4. Decide which devices should be gated behind clout tiers vs. freely purchasable (currently all `published` → all in the shop).

## Not done here (by design)

Real brand names, true list prices, and cited dimensions are intentionally omitted — those are your data-entry gate. These rows are scaffolding so the shop/sim-lab/compat engine have a full armamentarium to work with; replace-in-place as you cite.
